title: ficus malayana
intro: Ficus malayana is a plant species that belongs to the fig family (Moraceae) and is native to Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Ficus malayana has been used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting immunomodulatory effects. Ficus malayana is often used to make herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
